overview: The Village of Weston Board of Trustees approved multiple ordinances including rezoning requests and speed limit changes, though the Western Avenue speed limit ordinance was initially rejected then amended to keep Von Kennel to Highway J at 45 mph instead of reducing to 35 mph. The board also approved a 10-year baseball/softball field maintenance agreement, park fees, and various infrastructure contracts while deferring the remote meeting attendance policy to the next meeting when new board members will be seated.

agenda.item: Call to order and Pledge of Allegiance
Roll call - Cronin excused, all others present
Public comments - Jim Pensel on fire department funding
Minutes approval and acknowledgment of committee reports
Department reports - Administrator, Clerk, Finance, Fire/EMS, Parks, Planning, Police, Public Works, Technology
Ordinances - Rezoning and speed limit changes
Resolution - Hinter Springs Second Edition subdivision final plat
Unfinished business - April 2026 referendum informational sessions update
New business - E-bike ordinance, parking restrictions, intersection signage, field agreements, contracts
Remote meeting attendance policy and Microsoft Teams communication

discussions.body: Jim Pensel of 5002 Aerrol Street spoke critically about the board's approach to fire department staffing. After attending SAFER's citizen academy, he stated the department is 'severely understaffed' and criticized the upcoming referendum approach as 'kicking the can down the road' since it has no sunset date. He urged the board to prioritize fire/EMS funding over 'wants' like artificial turf and the aquatic center.

discussions.item: Finance Director Response to Public Comment
discussions.body: Finance Director Jessica responded that the village cannot borrow for additional firefighters - only for capital projects like the Kennedy Park turf field. She emphasized the village needs additional operating revenue, which is why a referendum is necessary. She expressed frustration, noting the village is 'the cheapest' and 'most efficient' but cannot fund more staff, and mentioned her position might be open in a couple months.

Fire Chief reported 28 calls during the blizzard with some calls requiring mutual aid due to staffing shortages. Public Works Director Michael reported staff worked 17-18 hour days, equipment held up well, and praised innovative approaches like using swap loaders for snow removal. Trustee Mark Maloney commended the crews and noted positive feedback from neighbors. The village's vac truck sold at auction for $352,000, exceeding the expected $250,000.

The original ordinance to set Western Avenue speed limits at 35 mph from Von Kennel to Ryan failed 4-3, with Maloney, Jordan, and the President voting no. Trustee Maloney argued the road conditions don't warrant 35 mph compared to other village roads. An amended motion was then approved to keep Von Kennel to Highway J at 45 mph while setting Von Kennel to Camp Phillips at 35 mph, with all other speed limit changes in the ordinance remaining intact.

The board approved a 10-year agreement for youth baseball and softball organizations to maintain fields at Kennedy Park. The lengthy term was justified because the organizations are investing money in the park and the agreement formalizes longstanding informal arrangements. The agreement specifies the village determines when fields can be used.

discussions.item: Intersection Signage at Community Center Drive and Birch Street
discussions.body: Trustee Hooang raised concerns about cyclists coming off the pedestrian bridge at high speeds, noting drivers might not stop for them. The board approved changing the stop sign to a yield sign as recommended by Community Life and Public Safety Committee, with a friendly amendment adding a stop sign for bicyclists on the path. Motion passed unanimously.

Trustee Lewis recommended deferring this item until the next meeting so the newly seated board can make the decision. The board voted unanimously to defer to the April 21st meeting when new board members will be in attendance.

The board approved using Microsoft Teams for trustee communications starting with the next term. Discussion clarified trustees can have multiple Teams profiles and that a training session will be held when the new board is seated. Email will still be available but Teams would replace text messaging between trustees.

publicComment: Jim Pensel of 5002 Aerrol Street spoke for approximately 4 minutes criticizing the board's approach to fire department funding. He praised SAFER staff after attending their citizen academy but argued the referendum approach is flawed because it has no sunset date and won't address future cost increases. He urged the board to prioritize fire/EMS over discretionary spending like artificial turf and the aquatic center.
actionItems: Minutes from February 16th meeting approved unanimously
Rezoning of 8905 Bert Street from RR5 to SFS approved unanimously
Rezoning of 7105 Christensen Avenue from SL to SFS approved unanimously
Speed limit ordinance amended and approved: Von Kennel to Camp Phillips at 35 mph, Von Kennel to Highway J remains 45 mph
Hinter Springs Second Edition subdivision final plat approved unanimously
E-bike/e-scooter ordinance tabled pending county completion of their process
No parking restrictions removed on west side of Alderson Street along Kennedy Park
Community Center Drive stop sign changed to yield sign with bicycle stop sign added on path
10-year baseball/softball field maintenance agreement approved
Commercial rotary mower purchase approved
Park shelter fees and field rental costs approved
Eagle Scout project at McKiller Park approved with funding from park operations
Remote meeting attendance policy deferred to April 21st meeting
Microsoft Teams approved for trustee communication starting next term
Military Road utility engineering service contract approved
Business 51 storm pond engineering service contract amendment ($13,500) approved
Sewer televising software contract approved
2026 annual stream maintenance plan budget approved
Hospital area repaving change order number four approved
Well rehabilitation approved
Sign encroachment agreement with Seventh Floor Investments LLC approved
Two remaining referendum Q&A sessions scheduled: March 31st 4:30-6:00 PM and April 2nd 12:00-1:30 PM
Next meeting April 21st at 6 PM with new board members
title: Board of Trustees
